svg.title: AWS Architecture: Lambda functions inside VPC connecting to EC2 via private IP
svg.desc: Diagram showing API Gateway outside the VPC routing to Lambda functions that are VPC-attached, which connect to EC2 running Spring Boot via private IP address inside the same VPC.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does a client call API Gateway?')
sendPrompt('Is API Gateway inside or outside the VPC?')
sendPrompt('How do I configure a Lambda function to run inside a VPC?')
sendPrompt('How do I configure a Lambda function to run inside a VPC?')
sendPrompt('How do I configure a Lambda function to run inside a VPC?')
sendPrompt('How do I configure a Lambda function to run inside a VPC?')
sendPrompt('How do I configure a Lambda function to run inside a VPC?')
sendPrompt('How does EC2 receive requests from Lambda via private IP?')
sendPrompt('How do I configure EC2 security groups to allow Lambda traffic on port 8080?')
sendPrompt('How does Spring Boot REST API serve requests on EC2?')
